skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Creates or updates AI skills by guiding scope definition, reusable resource planning, SKILL.md writing, and packaging validation.
skill.summary: Guide skill authors from idea to packaged AI skill.
skill.author: CesarAugustusGroB

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-severity weak-cryptography and Ruby backtick patterns, but review found these are Markdown prose, inline code names, and examples rather than executable cryptography or Ruby. The remaining real concern is moderate: the skill instructs agents to run local skill initialization and packaging scripts, which requires trust in the local workspace scripts.

medium_findings.title: Local Script Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to run initialization and packaging scripts. This is a legitimate skill-authoring workflow, but an agent should inspect or trust the local scripts before execution, especially because this package only includes SKILL.md.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command guidance is explicit and appears intended for legitimate local helper scripts. Confidence is moderate because no bundled script contents exist here to inspect for safety.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings point to Markdown code fences, inline file names, and directory examples. They are not Ruby code execution in this skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged regions are clearly Markdown examples and inline formatting. No Ruby source file or Ruby execution context exists in the reviewed package.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography findings appear to match ordinary text such as SKILL.md references, descriptions, and Markdown examples. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found instructional prose and Markdown metadata guidance at the cited locations. There is no cryptographic algorithm call, implementation, or recommendation.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Writing Guidance False Positives
low_findings.description: The static reconnaissance findings refer to skill organization guidance and asking users focused questions. The only grep mention is advice for searching large reference files, not host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain documentation practices, not commands that enumerate the host or extract system details. The grep reference is scoped to skill reference files.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell command examples for running local helper scripts. This pattern is acceptable for skill development, but marketplace users should treat those scripts as executable code requiring review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are visible and direct. The risk depends on the local scripts present in the user's workspace.

content.value_statement: Creating reusable AI skills can be inconsistent and hard to package. This skill guides scope, resources, instructions, and validation for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md structure and frontmatter fields.
Guides authors through scope discovery using concrete usage examples.
Recommends when to add scripts, references, and assets.
Shows progressive disclosure patterns for keeping skills concise.
Describes initialization, editing, packaging, and iteration workflows.
Provides guidance for validating skill organization before packaging.
limitations: Does not include the helper scripts referenced in its instructions.
Does not create a skill automatically without an agent editing files.
Does not validate marketplace policy or licensing terms.
Does not provide domain-specific templates beyond general skill patterns.

use_cases.title: Create a New Skill
use_cases.description: Define triggers, resources, and SKILL.md content for a new reusable assistant workflow.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Skill
use_cases.description: Review an existing skill for concise instructions, useful resources, and clear activation metadata.

use_cases.title: Prepare Skill Packaging
use_cases.description: Check structure, examples, and packaging steps before distributing a completed skill.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a skill for this task: [describe the recurring task]. Ask only the key questions needed to define the skill.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Skill Resources
prompt_templates.scenario: Author deciding what files belong in a skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these example user requests and recommend which instructions, scripts, references, or assets the skill should include.

prompt_templates.title: Refine SKILL.md
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SKILL.md for trigger clarity, progressive disclosure, unnecessary detail, and missing references.

prompt_templates.title: Package Readiness Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this skill folder for packaging readiness, file organization, resource references, testing needs, and final cleanup tasks.

output_examples.input: I want a skill that helps with recurring PDF rotation tasks.
output: A concise skill scope focused on PDF rotation.
A recommendation to include a tested local helper script.
A SKILL.md outline with trigger language and usage steps.

output_examples.input: Review this skill before I package it.
output: A readiness checklist covering metadata, resource references, and extra files.
Specific cleanup recommendations for unused example folders.
Packaging and validation steps to run before distribution.

best_practices: Start from concrete user requests before writing the skill body.
Keep SKILL.md concise and move detailed material into referenced files.
Inspect and test any scripts before relying on them in a packaged skill.
anti_patterns: Do not add broad documentation files that do not support the skill workflow.
Do not hide trigger conditions only inside the skill body.
Do not include unused example assets, scripts, or references in the final package.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create or update AI skills with clear triggers, concise instructions, and reusable resources.

faq.question: Does it generate files by itself?
faq.answer: No. It gives workflow guidance that an agent can follow while editing a skill folder.

faq.question: Why does it mention scripts?
faq.answer: It recommends scripts for repeatable tasks that need reliable execution, such as packaging or file processing.

faq.question: Is it safe to run the referenced scripts?
faq.answer: Only run scripts after reviewing their contents or confirming they come from a trusted source.

faq.question: Can it be used with Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is useful for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code skill authoring workflows.

faq.question: What should authors remove before packaging?
faq.answer: Remove unused example files, unused folders, and documentation that does not support the skill workflow.
